hawkish_phrases: inflation remained far too high and would stay above target for an extended period
risks to the inflation outlook were primarily on the upside
increasing risk that inflation might become entrenched and that second-round effects and a wage-price spiral could emerge
the current setting of the ECB's key policy rates was judged to be still accommodative, which implied that they should be increased further
monetary tightening would probably need to continue after the monetary policy stance had been normalised and moved into broadly neutral territory
dovish_phrases: economic activity was likely to have slowed significantly in the third quarter of the year and was expected to weaken further
risks to the economic growth outlook were clearly on the downside, especially in the near term
a technical recession was becoming the baseline scenario and the most likely outcome
the weakening of the economy could lead to somewhat higher unemployment in the future
a decline in energy costs and a further weakening of demand would lower price pressures
summary: The ECB raised rates by 75 basis points (with very large majority support, a few preferring 50bp) and recalibrated TLTRO III, emphasizing that inflation remained far too high, policy rates were still accommodative, and further rate increases were expected despite a deteriorating growth outlook and likely recession.
